# Evidence Brief - Economy & poverty (SDG 1, 8): Kenya

*Generated by CEDAR on 2026-06-20T14:52:40Z | Glass-box mode: every figure is cited below.*

## Key findings

- [-] Poverty rate ($3.00/day, 2021 PPP) rose from 44.6 to 45.5 % of population between 2005 and 2022 (+2.0%, +0.1%/yr). [1]
- [!] As of 2022, the value of 45.5 does not yet meet the benchmark of 3 (SDG 1.1: end extreme poverty (proxy <3% at $3.00/day)); a gap of 42.5 % of population remains. [2]
- [+] GDP per capita rose from 1092 to 1943 current US$ between 2010 and 2023 (+77.9%, +4.5%/yr). [3]
- [-] Unemployment rate rose from 2.8 to 5.4 % of labour force between 2010 and 2023 (+92.9%, +5.2%/yr). [4]

## Data confidence & caveats

- **Poverty rate ($3.00/day, 2021 PPP)** - confidence: **Low** (coverage 28%, latest 2022).
    - Caveat: Sparse series: only 5 of 18 years carry a value (survey-dependent indicator). Treat year-on-year movement with caution.
- **GDP per capita** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).
- **Unemployment rate** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).

## Methods & reproducibility

All figures are pulled from authoritative APIs and computed deterministically (trend = first vs latest observation; CAGR = compound annual rate; gap = distance to published benchmark). No figure is model-generated. Re-run any number with the query URLs in the evidence ledger below.
